name: Unencrypted Remote Access
description: Telnet (23) detected. Traffic is sent in cleartext, including passwords.
remediation: Disable Telnet and use SSH (Port 22) for encrypted management.

name: Legacy File Transfer
description: FTP (21) detected. FTP is insecure and prone to credential sniffing.
remediation: Replace FTP with SFTP or HTTPS for secure file transfers.

name: Exposed SMB Shares
description: SMB (445) detected. This is a high-value target for ransomware and lateral movement.
remediation: Ensure SMB is not exposed to the public and use SMB 3.0+ with encryption.

name: Stealth Hardware
description: The device is using a randomized MAC address.
remediation: Disable 'Private WiFi Address' in device settings for trusted networks.

name: Obscure Vendor
description: The hardware manufacturer could not be identified via OUI lookup.
remediation: Verify the physical device identity to ensure no unauthorized hardware exists.

name: Database Port Exposed
description: A common database port (MySQL/Postgres) is open.
remediation: Restrict database access to specific IP addresses via firewall.

name: Untrusted Certificate
description: The device is using a self-signed TLS certificate.
remediation: Review the certificate details. If this is a critical infrastructure device, consider installing a CA-signed certificate.
